description: NOTE Applies only to the Aruba 6300 Switch Series. In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for the matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.13.1080, what caveat is documented for ACLs?

Feature Caveat: ACLs - NOTE Applies only to the Aruba 6300 Switch Series. In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for the matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

description: VSF stack formation is blocked when there are reserved autojoin interfaces (25, 26, 49, 50) in the stack topology.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.13.1080, what caveat is documented for Aruba CX Mobile App?
Feature Caveat: Aruba CX Mobile App - VSF stack formation is blocked when there are reserved autojoin interfaces (25, 26, 49, 50) in the stack topology.

description: The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp 1

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.13.1080, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp 1.

description: Symptom: Incorrect ACL sequence number is applied in control plane ACL's. Scenario: When an ACL with both comments and the log action are applied to the control plane, incorrect ACL sequence number is configured for ACE. Workaround: Remove ACL comments from the control plane.
symptom: Incorrect ACL sequence number is applied in control plane ACL's.
scenario: When an ACL with both comments and the log action are applied to the control plane, incorrect ACL sequence number is configured for ACE.
workaround: Remove ACL comments from the control plane.

description: Symptom: Switches are unable to sync with NTP server even though NTP server is reachable and responding to the client. Scenario: Connect to an NTP server that uses special characters for the reference ID field. The NTP client device will not be able to sync with the server and the error Delimiter for jitter error will be displayed. Workaround: Connect to an NTP server that doesn't uses special characters for the reference ID fields.

description: Symptom: Some ARP/Broadcast packets are incorrectly sent back to the originating port. Scenario: In certain situations where a UBT user's role changes rapidly (e.g., during a transition from MAC authentication to 802.1X within the same sub-second), broadcast packets may be observed returning to the same port from which they originated. Workaround: Perform a shutdown and no shutdown operation on the client-connected access port. NOTE If an ISSU upgrade is used, a system reboot is required to clear the status of the UBT clients.

description: Symptom: BGP session fails to establish. When BGP is configured with the AO (Authentication Option) command, the session remains stuck in the "Connect" state if either peer is rebooted. Scenario: This issue occurs when TCP Authentication Option (TCPAO) is configured with a peer-group. Workaround: Delete and re-apply the config or use individual peer instead of peer-group.
symptom: BGP session fails to establish. When BGP is configured with the AO (Authentication Option) command, the session remains stuck in the "Connect" state if either peer is rebooted.
scenario: This issue occurs when TCP Authentication Option (TCPAO) is configured with a peer-group.
workaround: Delete and re-apply the config or use individual peer instead of peer-group.

description: Symptom: An IPTV may experience a delay when changing channels. This problem may occur intermittently depending on the number of multicast entries. Scenario: The multicast entries present in the database need to be updated with the activity-status every 30 seconds. The activity-status need to be fetched from the hardware for all the multicast entries. This is taking 5-6 seconds of time in the customer network. So, the channel change, which is leading to the forwarding port change in the hardware, is pending behind the update of activity-status of multicast entries.

The activity-status need to be fetched from the hardware for all the multicast entries. This is taking 5-6 seconds of time in the customer network. So, the channel change, which is leading to the forwarding port change in the hardware, is pending behind the update of activity-status of multicast entries.

description: Symptom: An IPTV may experience a delay when changing channels. This problem may occur intermittently depending on the number of multicast entries. Scenario: The multicast entries present in the database need to be updated with the activity-status every 30 seconds. The activity-status need to be fetched from the hardware for all the multicast entries. This is taking 5-6 seconds of time in the customer network. So, the channel change, which is leading to the forwarding port change in the hardware, is pending behind the update of activity-status of multicast entries.
symptom: An IPTV may experience a delay when changing channels. This problem may occur intermittently depending on the number of multicast entries.
scenario: The multicast entries present in the database need to be updated with the activity-status every 30 seconds. The activity-status need to be fetched from the hardware for all the multicast entries. This is taking 5-6 seconds of time in the customer network. So, the channel change, which is leading to the forwarding port change in the hardware, is pending behind the update of activity-status of multicast entries.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.13.1080, what is the workaround for IGMP Bug 338204?
IGMP (Bug ID 338204): No workaround is documented in the release notes.

description: Symptom: There is a 30-second delay when updating VLAN access settings with snmpset and immediately checking with snmpget. Scenario: This happens when snmpset changes one OID and snmpget reads another OID before the cache updates. Workaround: Use the CLI for VLAN access configuration instead of snmp.
symptom: There is a 30-second delay when updating VLAN access settings with snmpset and immediately checking with snmpget.

description: Symptom: When attempting to use SNMP to set the VlanStaticName object, the VLAN name changes temporarily but then reverts back to the original name. Scenario: Attempting to use SNMP set on dot1q/ieee VlanStaticName. Workaround: Use the command-line interface (CLI) to change the VLAN name instead of using SNMP set.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.
